questions.question: What is the topic for Week 19?
questions.explanation: The topic listed for Lesson Nineteen is EFFECTIVE PLANNING.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 17?
questions.explanation: Stay Pure was the topic for Week 17.

questions.question: A man's heart plans his way, But the LORD directs his ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Proverbs 16:9) says 'But the LORD directs his steps.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Proverbs 16:9.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 19 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: A man’s heart plans his way, But the LORD directs his steps.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Proverbs 16:9.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Proverbs 16:9), what directs a man's steps after his heart plans his way?
questions.explanation: The verse states: 'But the LORD directs his steps.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, effective planning transforms abstract goals into what?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'It is a process that transforms abstract goals into actionable steps.'

questions.question: Which of these is NOT one of the characteristics of a plan mentioned in the introduction?
questions.explanation: The text says 'our plans must be measurable, specific, relevant, achievable, flexible and time-bound.' Unchanging is the opposite of flexible.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'FACTORS AFFECTING EFFECTIVE PLANNING'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what sets the direction, guides priorities and informs decision-making?
questions.explanation: Point 1 states: 'Vision sets the direction, guides priorities and informs decision-making...'

questions.question: What happens to plans without vision according to the Week 19 lesson?
options: B. They take twice as long
C. They are aimless and unmotivated
questions.explanation: Point 1 explicitly states: 'Without vision, plans are aimless and unmotivated.'

questions.question: Without specific and measurable goals, planning becomes vague and unproductive.
questions.explanation: Point 2 states exactly this: 'Without specific and measurable goals, planning becomes vague and unproductive.'

questions.question: Which factor of effective planning allows you to hand a job off to someone who can complete it successfully while you focus on more important tasks?
questions.explanation: Point 3 defines Delegation as allowing you 'to hand a job off to someone who can complete it successfully...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that a late start or procrastination often results in inefficiency, warning against laziness?
questions.explanation: Point 4 cites '(Proverbs 6:9-11 warns against laziness...)'

questions.question: Disobedience to God's instructions can ________ even the most well-thought-out plans.
questions.explanation: Point 6 states 'Disobedience to God’s instructions can derail even the most well-thought-out plans.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'BENEFITS OF EFFECTIVE PLANNING'.

questions.question: How does effective planning relieve stress according to the Week 19 lesson?
options: A. It gives you more money to solve problems
B. It forces others to do the work for you
C. It provides a structured approach, eliminating the overwhelming feeling of being unprepared
D. It removes the need to pray
questions.explanation: Point 2 says 'It provides a structured approach to tackling tasks. When plans are in place, the overwhelming feeling of being unprepared is eliminated.'

questions.question: Effective planning minimizes distractions and maximizes output.
questions.explanation: Point 3 states 'With priorities and steps defined, distractions are minimised and output is maximized.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding effective planning resulting in more opportunities, and availing time for other things outside the routines?
questions.explanation: Point 5 links 'avails you time for other things' to '(Ecclesiastes 3:1).'

questions.question: According to the Week 19 lesson, planning allows you to achieve results worthy of what?
questions.explanation: Point 4 says 'Planning allows you to achieve results worthy of commendation (Matthew 25:21).'

questions.question: The conclusion of the Week 19 lesson states that effective planning will help you reach your desired goal.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states: 'Effective planning will help you to reach your desired goal.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'For which of you, intending to build a tower, does not sit down first and count the cost, whether he has enough to finish it —'
questions.verse_text: For which of you, intending to build a tower, does not sit down first and count the cost, whether he has enough to finish it —
questions.explanation: This is verse 28 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (Luke 14:28), what must a person intending to build a tower do first?
options: C. Sit down first and count the cost
questions.explanation: Verse 28 says '...does not sit down first and count the cost...'

questions.question: According to Luke 14:29, what will happen if a person lays a foundation and is not able to finish?
options: B. All who see it will begin to mock him
C. His family will finish it for him
questions.explanation: Verse 29 says 'lest, after he has laid the foundation, and is not able to finish, all who see it begin to mock him'.

questions.question: According to Luke 14:30, what specific phrase will the mockers say?
options: A. 'This man was foolish and lost his money'
B. 'This man began to build and was not able to finish'
C. 'This man had no faith in God'
D. 'This man did not pray before he built'
questions.explanation: Verse 30 quotes the mockers: “saying, ‘This man began to build and was not able to finish’?”

questions.question: What is Delegation said to prevent in Outline I?
questions.explanation: Point 3 concludes with: 'Delegation prevents burnout.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited as an example for delegation in Outline I?
questions.explanation: Point 3 cites '(Exodus 18:13-23)' for Delegation.

questions.question: Trusting in God to guide your efforts and provide resources is part of the 'Faith' factor in effective planning.
questions.explanation: Point 5 says 'Faith: It plays a significant role in planning... Trust in God to guide your efforts and provide the resources needed...'

questions.question: Which of these is NOT listed as something effective planning helps avoid in day-to-day activities?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'burn out, stress, frustration, depression, non-productivity, etc.' Physical sickness is not explicitly mentioned.

questions.question: Which scripture is associated with obedience, which is key to aligning plans with God's will?
questions.explanation: Point 6 cites '(1 Samuel 15:22-23)' for Obedience.

questions.question: In addition to the listed factors, effective planning must be done with God's guidance, wisdom, flexibility and right ________.
questions.explanation: The summary paragraph says 'must be done with God’s guidance... flexibility and right priorities (Matthew 6:33).'

questions.question: Beginning the day late has no effect on planning, as long as you eventually do the work.
questions.explanation: Point 4 says 'A late start or procrastination often results in inefficiency and missed opportunities.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that clear plans enable focused efforts, leading to maximum productivity?
questions.explanation: Point 3 of Outline II cites '(Proverbs 21:5)' regarding productivity.

questions.question: Which factor specifically provides focus and ensures that progress can be evaluated?
questions.explanation: Point 2 says 'Goals: ...Clear objectives provide focus and ensure that progress can be evaluated'.

questions.question: Effective planning involves not only managing time but also strategically allocating resources.
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'as it involves not only managing time but also strategically allocating resources...'
